title: Getting Started with World Of Taxonomy
description: API quickstart, MCP setup, authentication, and rate limits for the unified classification knowledge graph.

title: MCP Setup Guide for Claude Desktop, Cursor, Zed, and other AI clients
description: Step-by-step setup of the World Of Taxonomy MCP server in Claude Desktop, Cursor, Zed, VS Code Continue, Windsurf, and generic MCP clients. Covers API key generation, per-client config, verification, and troubleshooting.

title: Systems Catalog - All 1,000+ Classification Systems
description: Complete catalog of classification systems organized by category: industry, trade, occupation, health, education, financial, regulatory, and domain taxonomies.

title: Crosswalk Map - How Classification Systems Connect
description: Topology of 326K+ crosswalk edges linking classification systems. Translation paths, match types, and how to navigate between systems.

title: Industry Classification Guide - Which System to Use
description: Decision tree for choosing the right industry classification system by country, purpose, and granularity. Compares NAICS, ISIC, NACE, SIC, and regional systems.

title: Medical and Health Classification Systems Compared
description: Comparison of ICD-10-CM, ICD-11, MeSH, LOINC, ATC, SNOMED CT, and other health classification systems with use cases and crosswalk paths.

title: Trade and Product Classification Guide
description: How HS, CPC, UNSPSC, SITC, and BEC relate. Code structure, crosswalk paths, and which system to use for trade, procurement, and statistical reporting.

title: Occupation Classification Systems Compared
description: SOC vs ISCO vs ESCO vs O*NET compared. Structure, granularity, crosswalk paths, and which occupation system to use by country and purpose.

title: Categories and Sectors - How Systems Are Organized
description: The 16 categories and domain sectors that organize 1,000+ classification systems in World Of Taxonomy. Category definitions, system counts, and navigation.

title: Domain Taxonomies vs Official Standards
description: How World Of Taxonomy splits classification systems into domain taxonomies (plain-language on-ramps) and official standards (NAICS, ISIC, etc), and how every surface exposes the split.

title: Data Quality and Provenance
description: Four-tier provenance framework, source verification, hash auditing, and how to report data quality issues.

title: Inclusion Policy - Which Systems Belong in World Of Taxonomy
description: Governing policy for what qualifies as a World Of Taxonomy system: published external sources, stable identifiers, enumerated or hierarchical structure, practical size. Used by contributors and downstream portfolio products to decide whether a vocabulary or taxonomy belongs.

title: Web Vocabularies - schema.org, SKOS, and Related Type Systems
description: Type and concept vocabularies used by AI assistants, search engines, and structured-data crawlers. Covers schema.org (926 types) as the primary AEO/SEO anchor, plus SKOS, W3C standards, and IAB content taxonomy.

title: Regulatory and Compliance Standards - HIPAA, GDPR, NIST, ISO, OSHA, and 116 more
description: 120 regulatory and compliance frameworks: US federal (HIPAA, SOX, OSHA, FDA, NIST), US frameworks (NIST CSF, SOC 2, PCI DSS, US GAAP), EU directives (GDPR, NIS2, DORA, MDR, EU AI Act, CSRD), ISO management standards (9001, 14001, 27001, 22301, 45001, 13485, 42001), and global treaties (Basel III, FATF, ILO, Paris Agreement, IMO MARPOL/SOLAS). Cross-framework overlap guide for compliance teams and GRC tooling.

title: System Architecture and Data Flows
description: Architecture diagrams showing system design, data ingestion pipeline, API request flow, MCP session lifecycle, and the four-channel wiki distribution.
